user.title: Energy Sources
user.description: Step 1/2: Connect AURUM to your energy sensors.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your grid meter (e.g. Shelly 3EM, SMA, Fronius). Must report watts — positive = importing from grid, negative = exporting to grid. This is the most important sensor.
data_description.pv_power_entity: Your solar inverter's current production in watts. Optional — only used for display, not for surplus calculation.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Your home battery's state of charge in percent. Leave empty if you don't have a battery — AURUM will distribute all surplus directly.

battery.title: Battery & System Settings
battery.description: Step 2/2: Configure how AURUM handles your battery and update frequency.

data_description.battery_capacity_wh: Usable capacity of your battery in watt-hours (e.g. 10000 for a 10 kWh battery).
data_description.target_soc: Charge goal in %. Above this level, all devices are allowed to run. Below it, only devices whose individual SOC threshold is met.
data_description.min_soc: Safety floor in %. Below this, ALL devices are turned off to protect the battery from deep discharge.
data_description.update_interval: How often AURUM reads sensors and adjusts devices (in seconds). 15s is recommended for most setups.

data.action: What would you like to do?

settings.title: Energy & Battery Settings
settings.description: Update your energy sources and battery parameters.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your grid meter in watts. Positive = import, negative = export.
data_description.pv_power_entity: Solar inverter production in watts (optional, for display only).
data_description.target_soc: Above this SOC, all devices are allowed. Below, per-device thresholds apply.
data_description.min_soc: Below this SOC, ALL devices are turned off to protect the battery.
data_description.update_interval: Sensor polling and device control interval in seconds.

add_device.title: Add Device
add_device.description: Add a device to be controlled by AURUM. Any smart plug or switch in Home Assistant works.

data_description.name: A friendly name like 'Washing Machine' or 'Pool Pump'. Used for sensor names in Home Assistant.
data_description.switch_entity: The smart plug or switch that controls this device (e.g. switch.shelly_plug_washer). Any switch.* or input_boolean.* entity works.
data_description.power_entity: A sensor measuring this device's real-time power draw in watts. If not set, the nominal power value is used instead.
data_description.nominal_power: Expected power consumption in watts when the device is running. Used for surplus calculations and as fallback when no power sensor is set.
data_description.priority: Higher priority devices get surplus power first. Use 80-100 for essential loads (washing machine), 50-70 for nice-to-have (pool pump), 20-40 for optional (EV charger).
data_description.soc_threshold: When battery SOC drops below the target, this device will only run if SOC is still above this threshold. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.startup_detection: Enable for appliances that run programs (washing machines, dishwashers, dryers). When a program starts, AURUM protects it from being interrupted — even if surplus drops to zero.
data_description.deadline: Time by which this device should have finished its cycle (e.g. 18:00). If the remaining time before the deadline is less than the estimated runtime, AURUM will force-start the device regardless of surplus. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.estimated_runtime: How long a typical program takes in minutes (e.g. 120 for a 2-hour wash cycle). Used together with deadline to calculate when to force-start.

remove_device.title: Remove Device
remove_device.description: Select a device to remove from AURUM control.
